label: Reproducibility, drift & continuous monitoring

node.label: Reproducibility, drift & continuous monitoring
node.definition: Reproducibility and continuous monitoring track versions, seeds, data, metrics, drift, incidents, and operational health across experiments and deployments.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Reproducibility, drift & continuous monitoring is to connect Checkpointing, recovery & experiment state, Benchmarks, test suites & task metrics to downstream capabilities including Evaluation-driven development & release gates, Observability, tracing & evaluation harnesses, Model registry, versioning & MLOps / LLMOps.
examples: substrate for Evaluation-driven development & release gates
substrate for Observability, tracing & evaluation harnesses
substrate for Model registry, versioning & MLOps / LLMOps
limitations: Coverage is necessarily incomplete: measured proxies, policies, and test suites cannot enumerate every deployment context or adversary. Its direct dependencies include Checkpointing, recovery & experiment state, Benchmarks, test suites & task metrics.

dependencies.rationale: Reproducibility, drift & continuous monitoring measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Checkpointing, recovery & experiment state.
Reproducibility, drift & continuous monitoring measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Benchmarks, test suites & task metrics.
Reproducibility, drift & continuous monitoring measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Distributed systems & networking.
